query: My admin's top nav has like 9 section tabs and on a phone they overflow off-screen and shove the logo out of view. I was going to add flex-wrap so they stack onto two rows on mobile. Good call?
expected_behavior: Rejects flex-wrap / stacking tabs onto multiple rows for an overflowing tab bar
Prescribes a single horizontal-scroll line: container gets flex flex-nowrap overflow-x-auto plus min-w-0 (optionally md:overflow-x-visible), each item gets shrink-0 whitespace-nowrap
Explains min-w-0 is the key: a flex item defaults to min-width:auto so without it the tab row pushes its neighbors (logo) off the viewport instead of scrolling
Frames horizontal scroll as the default for any growable tab bar / segmented control (Gmail / Instagram / Twitter pattern)
notes: Guards the wrong instinct of wrapping an overflowing mobile tab row onto stacked rows. The skill knows the canonical fix is a single horizontal-scroll line and that min-w-0 is the load-bearing class preventing siblings from being shoved off-screen.
